llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Selected gpt_plus_overview over gpt_only because it represents the most capable AI-assisted condition (GPT-4 with additional prompt engineering training), achieving a higher completion rate (93% vs 91%).
llm_extracted.notes: Sample sizes (n) are not provided in the arms data. The effect_in_outcome_units field reports '12.2% increase in completion rates' but this appears to be a rounded approximation; the actual difference between means is (0.93-0.82)/0.82 = 13.4%. No standard deviations, standard errors, or confidence intervals are reported. Task completion rate is a throughput-type measure (percentage of tasks completed), so larger values indicate better performance.
source_quotes: The control group completed on average 82% of their tasks; those in the GPT + overview group completed about 93%; and those in the GPT-only group completed about 91%.

computed.notes: Sample sizes (n) are not provided in the arms data.
